# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Docker Compose configuration for RabbitMQ deployment. Define RabbitMQ service with management plugin enabled. Expose management UI port for monitoring and administration. Configure environment variables for cluster setup. This is for our production message broker infrastructure.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent rabbitmq management exposed.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: rabbitmq_management_exposed

version: '3'  # Define the version of docker compose file format to be used (v2)
services:   # Defines services in your application which are managed by Docker Compose and run on one machine or more machines as a single service. Each definition is made up from three parts - Services, Links & Options – all separated with dashes '---' at the top of each file (the version).
   rabbitmq: # Name your services here for easy reference in docker-compose command line arguments and yaml files that include them. For example if you have two service named "web" and another one called web2, they will be accessible as 'services' under the main file (docker compose).
     image: rabbitmq # Specify Docker Image to use for your RabbitMQ Service here which is already included in docker-compose.yml by default if not specified otherwise it uses official images of Rabbit MQ from Docker Hub as per requirement provided above, but you can also specify custom image or build one using a Dockerfile
     ports: # Specify the port mappings for your service here (mandatory) – this is where we expose our application to outside world. For example if it's set up on host machine and exposed at 'localhost', then access will be available via http://127.0.0.1
       - "5672:5672" # RabbitMQ default port for AMQP protocol is 5672, you can change it as per requirement if not using the defaults provided here but must match with your application's requirements otherwise connection will fail in case of mismatch.
     environment: - RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_USER=user # Define Environment Variables for Rabbit Mq Service (mandatory) – this is where we set our user and password which are used by the client applications to connect with rabbitmq server, must match in both application's requirements.
       - RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_PASS=password # Define Environment Variables for Rabbit Mq Service (mandatory) – this is where we set our user and password which are used by the client applications to connect with rabbitmq server, must match in both application's requirements.
     volumes: - ./data:/var/lib/rabbitmq # This will mount a directory from your host machine into RabbitMQ container at /var/lib/rabbitmq (mandatory) – this is where we store our data persistently, so it can survive if the rabbit service crashes or restarts.
     depends_on: - rabbitmgmt # This will ensure that 'Rabbit Mgmt' starts before RabbitMQ server start up and running (mandatory) – this is to make sure our application wait till RABBITMGMT has started, so it can access the management UI.
     restart: unless-stopped - Specify how Docker should respond when a container stops or restarts here if not specified otherwise docker will try its best but you have control over that (mandatory) – this is to ensure your application always running and listening on port as per requirement provided above, must match with the host machine's requirements.
secrets: # Define secrets in a Docker Compose file for secure storage of sensitive data like database password or API keys which should not be exposed (optional) – this is to prevent exposing these details via plaintext logs and command line arguments if required by your application’s requirement, must match with the host machine's requirements.
